homepage.title: Verified public information and confidential reporting
homepage.introduction: The Garkuwa Platform is being developed to support verified news and public updates alongside a separate channel for confidential incident reporting. These services are not live yet.
homepage.pillarsIntroduction: Information published for the public will remain separate from confidential reports reviewed by internal staff.
homepage.newsDescription: Announcements, advisories, warnings and public updates will be published after following a verification process.
homepage.reportingDescription: A dedicated form will allow citizens to share incident information without creating an account.
homepage.trustIntroduction: The planned workflow reduces the personal information requested, but it does not make an absolute promise that risk can never occur.
homepage.trustAccounts: Citizen accounts will not be required to submit a report.
homepage.trustContact: Follow-up contact details will be optional.
homepage.trustTracking: Reports will not have a public tracking portal.
homepage.trustSeparation: Public information and confidential reports will follow separate workflows.
homepage.stepOneDescription: Review verified news, announcements and public advisories.
homepage.stepTwoDescription: Share relevant information through the confidential form once it is available.
homepage.stepThreeDescription: Internal staff will review information through the administration system and take an appropriate next step.
homepage.linksDescription: Learn how the platform is planned, what to expect and where to find general help.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.introduction: Answers about the platform's purpose and the services being planned.

items.question: What is the Garkuwa Platform?
items.answer: It is a platform being developed for verified public information and a separate channel for confidential incident reports.

items.question: Why is Hausa the default language?
items.answer: Hausa is primary so information is easier to access for communities that use it in everyday life.

items.question: Is English supported?
items.answer: Yes. English pages are provided in the same application, while Hausa remains the primary public language.

items.question: Will citizens need accounts?
items.answer: No. The planned reporting workflow will not require a citizen account.

items.question: Will contact details be mandatory?
items.answer: No. Providing contact details for possible follow-up will be optional.

items.question: Can a report be tracked publicly?
items.answer: No. A public report-tracking portal is not planned.

items.question: How will confidential information be handled?
items.answer: The workflow is designed to limit requested information and keep reports separate from published content. Safeguards will continue to be reviewed as the service is developed.

items.question: How is public content different from incident reports?
items.answer: Public information will be verified before publication. Incident reports will enter an internal review workflow and will not be published automatically.

items.question: What should someone do during an immediate emergency?
items.answer: Do not wait for this platform. Contact the appropriate emergency services for the area where the emergency is happening.

items.question: Are the news and reporting services live?
items.answer: No. Both are under development and will be clearly identified when they become available.

help.title: Help
help.introduction: This page explains the available public pages and what the reporting process is expected to look like after launch.

sections.title: Using the public site
sections.body: Use the primary navigation to return home or open questions, help, about and contact pages. News and reporting services are not live yet.

sections.title: Changing language
sections.body: Choose Hausa or English in the header or footer. You will be taken to the equivalent version of the page you are reading.

sections.title: The future reporting process
sections.body: After the form launches, a reporter will describe what happened and internal staff will review the information. An account will not be required.

sections.title: Information to prepare
sections.body: Prepare a short account of what was observed, when and where it happened, and why it may be important. Avoid adding personal information that is not relevant.

sections.title: Safe and responsible reporting
sections.body: Share what is known without inventing allegations or putting yourself or another person at risk. Do not upload passwords, unnecessary identity documents, or files you are not permitted to share.

sections.title: Emergencies and general support
sections.body: This platform is not a replacement for emergency services. For general support, use the official details once confirmed on the Contact us page.

help.reportingInactive: The confidential reporting form is not active during this phase.

about.title: About the Garkuwa Platform
about.introduction: This platform is being developed to help people access useful public information and share incident information through an appropriate channel.

sections.title: Hausa first, with English support
sections.body: Hausa is the primary language to improve access to information. English is supported in the same application rather than a separate product.

sections.title: Separate workflows
sections.body: Content intended for publication will follow a verification workflow. Confidential reports will follow a separate internal review workflow.

sections.title: Responsible operational use
sections.body: The platform is intended to support useful work and collaboration with public stakeholders in Nigeria without claiming a formal government endorsement.

sections.title: Incremental delivery
sections.body: The foundation and informational pages are being delivered first. Additional services will be introduced in stages after further review and testing.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.introduction: Confirmed Garkuwa Foundation contact details will be published here after approval.
contact.sensitiveWarningTitle: Do not send sensitive information through general contact channels
contact.sensitiveWarning: Do not send incident evidence, confidential information or emergency requests through these channels. A dedicated confidential reporting form will be provided separately.
contact.noForm: No contact form or direct email integration is available during this phase.

footer.summary: A Hausa-first platform being developed for verified public information and confidential reporting.
footer.emergencyText: Do not wait for this platform. Contact the appropriate emergency services in your area.
footer.ownership: Garkuwa Foundation. This public platform is provided for community information.

trust.title: Trust and confidentiality notice
trust.body: The planned reporting workflow is designed to limit requested information. Do not send sensitive information through ordinary contact channels.

featureStatus.notActive: This service is not active yet.
